<!-- ═══════════════ ABOUT ═══════════════ -->

```console
abid@redwan:~$ cat about.md
```

I live at the seam of **AI engineering**, **human-centred design**, and **physical computing** —
where most people pick one lane, I merge all three into systems that *feel inevitable* to use.
The work I'm proudest of isn't the cleverest model; it's the system a non-technical user forgets
is even there.

> *"The best brief I've ever gotten: 'We have a problem nobody's solved yet.'"*

<!-- ═══════════════ PRINCIPLES ═══════════════ -->

```console
abid@redwan:~$ cat ~/.principles
```

```text
01  the best interface is no interface — the 2nd best gets out of the way fast
02  latency is UX — sub-second, or it doesn't feel alive
03  ship it — a rough build in prod beats a perfect deck
04  AI should remove decisions, not add them
05  if it needs a manual, the design already failed
06  automate the boring — good systems work while you sleep
```
